generated_by: LITMUS discovery-study reducer (DESIGN §17 step 4, caveat). The subset of catalogued claims concrete enough to be benchmark items. control_signal_note: DESIGN §17 sanity check: do suspect (candidate-flawed) claims concentrate in the positive-control (retracted) papers vs the negative-control (registered-report) papers? ANSWER: NO, and the reason is structural. (1) The two POSITIVE controls (biology-anon-retracted-oblongifolin, biology-anon-retracted-stk39) are 1-page RETRACTION NOTICES, not the original articles — they contain no figures/tables/statistics to audit, so their flaws are integrity SIGNALS (declared image/data duplication, route-to-human) rather than recomputable suspects. The retracted DATA is not even present in the corpus. (2) The two NEGATIVE controls behave oppositely to expectation at the deterministic frontier: druckman2021 (registered report) is clean (no fabricated errors, all anchors supported), but anon2024-rr (registered report) carries a CLUSTER of statcheck/CI/df transcription errors (c1-c7) despite its central preregistered null being internally sound. (3) Checkable suspect claims therefore concentrate in the 'none'-control papers — wheeler2025, lin2025, gkekas2025, anchuri2026, singh2026, the chemistry set — not in the controls. CAVEAT: these are reasoner CANDIDATE judgments, so this is a signal on the cataloguing/control design, NOT a verdict on any paper.
caveat: candidate labels — reasoner judgments, not gold until a deterministic verifier or human confirms (DESIGN §17)
